meaning: in a general way / generally speaking
communicative_purpose: Introducing general statements or broad perspectives
trigger_situations: Presenting overview arguments, generalizing observations, opening presentations, framing discussions
contexts: Business meetings, academic writing, formal presentations, professional reports, structured debates
variations: d'une manière générale, de manière générale, en règle générale, d'une façon générale
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'en général' (different register); using without proper context introduction; placing too late in argument
collocates: d'une manière générale, dans l'ensemble, de façon générale, généralement parlant
example_2: Tu peux dire d'une manière générale quand tu veux exprimer in a general way / generally speaking.
example_3: On utilise d'une manière générale dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say in a general way when you want to express in a general way / generally speaking.
example_3_translation: It is generally used in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Correcting a misconception or adding clarifying information
trigger_situations: Clarifying misunderstandings, providing accurate information, contradicting expectations, adding nuance
contexts: Discussions, debates, explanations, casual conversations, professional settings
variations: en fait, en réalité, en vérité, en fait pas du tout
common_mistakes: Using as simple emphasis instead of correction; confusing with 'en effet' (which confirms); overusing in formal writing
collocates: en fait, mais en fait, non en fait, en fait c'est
example_2: Tu peux dire en fait quand tu veux exprimer in fact / actually.
example_3: On utilise en fait dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say in fact when you want to express in fact / actually.
example_3_translation: We actually use it in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Expressing personal viewpoint while acknowledging subjectivity
trigger_situations: Giving personal perspectives, discussing controversial topics, sharing interpretations, stating beliefs
contexts: Debates, essays, meetings, casual discussions, formal arguments
variations: à mon avis, de mon point de vue, selon moi, pour ma part
common_mistakes: Forgetting 'à' preposition; using in formal reports without attribution; confusing with 'je pense que'
collocates: à mon avis, à votre avis, à son avis, à notre avis
example_2: Tu peux dire à mon avis quand tu veux exprimer in my opinion.
example_3: On utilise à mon avis dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say in my opinion when you want to express in my opinion.
example_3_translation: In my opinion, it is used in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Introducing the first element of a balanced argument
trigger_situations: Presenting two sides of an issue, weighing pros and cons, structured argumentation, comparative analysis
contexts: Formal debates, academic writing, business presentations, strategic planning, analytical reports
variations: d'une part, d'un côté, premièrement, d'entrée de jeu
common_mistakes: Using without 'd'autre part' to complete the structure; awkward sentence construction when not followed by contrast
collocates: d'une part, d'autre part, d'une part... d'autre part, d'un côté... de l'autre
example_2: Tu peux dire d'une part quand tu veux exprimer on one hand.
example_3: On utilise d'une part dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say on the one hand when you want to express on one hand.
example_3_translation: On the one hand, we use it in everyday life.

trigger_situations: Ending lists, summarizing points, expressing exasperation, transitioning to main point
contexts: Conversations, presentations, formal discussions, narrative structure, casual speech
variations: enfin, en fin de compte, finalement, lastly, en dernier lieu
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'à la fin' (at the end of); using for emphasis without proper context; register mismatch in formal documents
collocates: enfin, et enfin, mais enfin, enfin bref, enfin presque
example_1: J'utilise souvent enfin pour parler de finally / well / at last.
example_2: Tu peux dire enfin quand tu veux exprimer finally / well / at last.
example_3: On utilise enfin dans la vie quotidienne.
example_1_translation: I often use finally to talk about finally / well / at last.
example_2_translation: You can say finally when you want to express finally / well / at last.
example_3_translation: Finally, we use it in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Clarifying, specifying, or providing precise definition
trigger_situations: Defining terms, giving examples, explaining technical concepts, elaborating on statements
contexts: Academic texts, professional meetings, technical explanations, formal writing, lectures
variations: c'est-à-dire, c'est-à-dire que, à savoir, autrement dit, soit dit
common_mistakes: Overusing as filler; confusing with 'c'est que'; pronouncing incorrectly (silent 'e'); using in place of 'c'est que'
collocates: c'est-à-dire, eh bien c'est-à-dire, ou plutôt c'est-à-dire
example_2: Tu peux dire c'est-à-dire quand tu veux exprimer that is to say / i.e..
example_3: On utilise c'est-à-dire dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say that is to say when you want to express that is to say / i.e..
example_3_translation: We use that is to say in daily life.

meaning: I am in the process of
communicative_purpose: Indicating an ongoing action in progress
trigger_situations: Explaining current activities, describing ongoing work, updating progress, excuse-making
contexts: Work updates, status reports, daily conversations, explaining delays, professional communication
variations: je suis en train de, je suis en train de faire, je me suis mis à, je suis en cours de
common_mistakes: Dropping 'en train'; using with past or future tense incorrectly; confusing with 'je vais' for future; informal contraction 'je suis entrain de' (incorrect)
collocates: je suis en train de, nous sommes en train de, je serais en train de
example_2: Tu peux dire je suis en train de quand tu veux exprimer I am in the process of.
example_3: On utilise je suis en train de dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say I am in the process of when you want to express I am in the process of.
example_3_translation: We use I'm trying to in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Limiting a statement to one's own viewpoint or domain
trigger_situations: Clarifying personal scope, distinguishing individual from general, expressing personal stance
contexts: Formal discussions, professional meetings, questionnaires, policy debates, academic discourse
variations: en ce qui me concerne, concernant, pour ce qui est de, en ce qui a trait à
common_mistakes: Confusing word order; using 'concerne' as verb instead of participle; overformal for casual contexts
collocates: en ce qui me concerne, en ce qui concerne, en ce qui nous concerne
example_2: Tu peux dire en ce qui me concerne quand tu veux exprimer as far as I'm concerned.
example_3: On utilise en ce qui me concerne dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say as far as I'm concerned when you want to express as far as I'm concerned.
example_3_translation: We use it in my daily life.

communicative_purpose: Acknowledging a point while introducing a contrasting perspective
trigger_situations: Balancing arguments, conceding points, adding nuance, transitional moments in discourse
contexts: Formal debates, professional reports, analytical writing, structured discussions
variations: cela dit, ceci dit, pourtant, néanmoins, toutefois
common_mistakes: Using without prior statement to contrast; placing at sentence beginning awkwardly; confusing with 'donc' (therefore)
collocates: cela dit, mais cela dit, et cela dit, toujours est-il que
example_2: Tu peux dire cela dit quand tu veux exprimer that said / having said that.
example_3: On utilise cela dit dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say ça dit when you want to express that said / having said that.
example_3_translation: We use this said in daily life.

meaning: so to speak / as it were
communicative_purpose: Softening statements, indicating figurative language, adding caution
trigger_situations: Using metaphors, generalizing loosely, self-correcting, diplomatic formulations
contexts: Formal speeches, diplomatic discourse, academic writing, professional negotiations
variations: pour ainsi dire, pour ainsi dire proverbial, comme qui dirait, si j'ose dire
common_mistakes: Using without figurative context; overusing in formal writing; placement errors in sentence structure
collocates: pour ainsi dire, comme on dit, si l'on peut dire, en quelque sorte
example_2: Tu peux dire pour ainsi dire quand tu veux exprimer so to speak / as it were.
example_3: On utilise pour ainsi dire dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say so to speak when you want to express so to speak / as it were.
example_3_translation: We use it so to speak in everyday life.

trigger_situations: Stating obvious facts, emphasizing shared knowledge, rhetorical emphasis, preamble to arguments
contexts: Formal writing, speeches, academic texts, professional reports, diplomatic discourse
variations: il va sans dire que, cela va sans dire, de soi-dit, évidemment
common_mistakes: Using without completing 'que'; overly dramatic in casual contexts; redundant with other emphasis words
collocates: il va sans dire que, cela va sans dire que, comme il va sans dire
example_2: Tu peux dire il va sans dire que quand tu veux exprimer it goes without saying that.
example_3: On utilise il va sans dire que dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say it goes without saying that when you want to express it goes without saying that.
example_3_translation: It goes without saying that we use it in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Confirming shared understanding or obvious agreement
trigger_situations: Affirming points, conceding minor issues, agreeing with conditions, acknowledging obvious facts
contexts: Business meetings, formal conversations, negotiations, professional discussions, polite discourse
variations: bien entendu, naturellement, bien sûr, cela va de soi, il est évident que
common_mistakes: Using sarcastically without context; confusing with 'évidemment' (which is stronger); repetition without substance
collocates: bien entendu, mais bien entendu, oui bien entendu, bien entendu que
example_2: Tu peux dire bien entendu quand tu veux exprimer of course / naturally.
example_3: On utilise bien entendu dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say of course when you want to express of course / naturally.
example_3_translation: We of course use it in everyday life.

trigger_situations: Confessing, being honest, revealing true feelings, self-reflection, critical assessment
contexts: Personal discussions, professional feedback, interviews, confessional contexts, honest evaluations
variations: à vrai dire, franchement, honnêtement, soit dit entre nous, entre nous soit dit
common_mistakes: Using to introduce controversial opinions without softening; confusing with 'en fait'; overusing in professional emails
collocates: à vrai dire, à dire vrai, pour dire vrai, en toute franchise
example_2: Tu peux dire à vrai dire quand tu veux exprimer to tell the truth / frankly.
example_3: On utilise à vrai dire dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can tell the truth when you want to express to tell the truth / frankly.
example_3_translation: We actually use it in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Adding information that supports or extends the previous point
trigger_situations: Building arguments, adding supporting evidence, continuing narratives, reinforcing points
contexts: Academic writing, formal reports, business proposals, structured arguments, presentations
variations: de plus, de surcroît, en outre, de plus, par ailleurs
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'du plus' (doesn't exist in this context); using in spoken language where 'aussi' would be natural; placement errors
collocates: de plus, et de plus, de plus en plus, qui plus est
example_2: Tu peux dire de plus quand tu veux exprimer moreover / furthermore.
example_3: On utilise de plus dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say more when you want to express more/more.
example_3_translation: We use it more in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Concluding with final assessment or summary
trigger_situations: Drawing conclusions, summarizing arguments, reaching final judgments, closing discussions
contexts: Formal debates, academic conclusions, strategic decisions, business summaries, legal contexts
variations: en définitive, en fin de compte, finalement, en dernière analyse, somme toute
common_mistakes: Using too early before presenting all arguments; confusing with 'définitivement' (permanently); overly formal for everyday speech
collocates: en définitive, en définitive il faut, mais en définitive
example_2: Tu peux dire en définitive quand tu veux exprimer ultimately / in short.
example_3: On utilise en définitive dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say ultimately when you want to express ultimately / in short.
example_3_translation: We ultimately use it in daily life.
